mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending supports economic growth
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are predominantly negative (less than 40% positive) over a 2-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a tightening labor market, indicating strong economic expansion and increased consumer purchasing power, which is bullish for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Low unemployment typically correlates with economic growth
failure_condition: If UNRATE consistently falls below 5.0% but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rates and rising housing starts indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting a booming housing market, which has positive spillover effects on the broader economy and equity markets.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 4.0% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market strength often leads economic cycles
failure_condition: If the specified conditions for FEDFUNDS and HOUST are met, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in over 60% of triggered periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production without significant inflationary pressure signals healthy supply-side expansion and efficient resource utilization, leading to improved corporate profitability and positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.5%
failure_condition: If industrial production accelerates while inflation remains low, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently negative (Rank IC < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation, as measured by CPI, rising above a 3.0% year-over-year threshold signals potential overheating in the economy, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation often leads to interest rate hikes
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently exceeds 3.0% year-over-year but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust and expanding job market, which directly supports consumer income and spending, leading to higher corporate profits and positive equity performance.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive growth but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are predominantly negative (less than 45% positive) over any 1-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of slowing GDP growth and aggressive interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve signals an economy struggling to maintain momentum under tightening financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 2.0% AND FEDFUNDS increased by > 0.5% in the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Rapid rate hikes can slow economic growth
failure_condition: If the specified conditions for GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS are met, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in over 55% of triggered periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts, particularly during a period of rising interest rates, indicates a weakening housing market and broader economic contraction, which typically precedes negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average decline > 10% AND FEDFUNDS has increased in the last 6 months
failure_condition: If HOUST shows a significant decline while FEDFUNDS is rising, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances, this hypothesis is falsified.
